lines.en_lit: cork on Yom Kippur? Rabbi Yitzḥak bar Naḥmani stood on his feet and said: I saw that Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi went out on Yom Kippur in cork sandals, and I said to him: What on a communal fast He said to me: no different, Rabba bar bar Ḥana said: I saw Rabbi Elazar from Nineveh go out in cork sandals on a communal fast day, and I said to him: What on Yom Kippur? He said to me: no different, Rav Yehuda went out in reed sandals. Abaye went out in palm fiber. Rava went out in reeds. Rabba bar Rav Huna wrapped a scarf around his feet and went out.

Rami bar Ḥama raised an objection: an amputee go out with his prosthetic leg these are the words of Rabbi Meir. But Rabbi Yosei prohibits it. And was taught in that regard And they agree that it is prohibited to go out wearing it on Yom Kippur. Abaye said: There, there are rags due pleasure

Rava said to him: And if is not a garment, rags make it into a garment? And furthermore, any pleasure that is not shoes, is it prohibited on Yom Kippur? And Rabba bar Rav Huna would wrap a scarf on his feet and go out, Furthermore, From that it teaches in the latter clause: If has a receptacle for rags it ritual impurity it may be inferred that in the first clause we are not dealing with has rags.

Rather, Rava said: Actually, according to everyone, is a shoe, and with regard to Shabbat this is what they disagree about: One Sage, holds that we decree lest slip off and one come to carry it four cubits and one Sage, holds that we do not decree

The Sages taught: Young children are permitted all of except for wearing shoes. What is different wearing shoes? because will say adults did this for him, those also, they will say adults did this for him, they say that they did this for him yesterday,

say shoes as well. They say that they did this for him yesterday. shoes, it is impossible did it for him yesterday, As Shmuel said: He who desires a taste of death should put on shoes and go to sleep.

But is teaching they are permitted ab initio. Rather, The Sages decreed those that are not for growth, the Sages did not decree those that are for growth. Abaye said: mother told me: A child’s growth hot water and oil he grows a little, egg with kutaḥa, When he grows a little vessels to break, Rabba bought cracked ceramic vessels for his children, and they broke them

the king and the bride wash their faces Who is the mishna? Rabbi Ḥananya ben Teradyon, as it was taught The king and the bride not wash their faces Rabbi Ḥananya ben Teradyon says in the name of Rabbi Eliezer: A king and a bride wash their faces. A new mother not wear shoes Rabbi Ḥananya ben Teradyon says in the name of Rabbi Eliezer: A new mother wear shoes.

what is the reason the king Because it is written: “Your eyes shall see the king in his beauty” What is the reason a bride So that she should not repulsive to her husband. Rav said to Rabbi Ḥiyya: For how long a bride? He said to him: As it was taught we do not prevent the bride from wearing perfumes the entire thirty days
A new mother wear shoes. Due to the cold
Shmuel said: If due to the danger of scorpions, he is permitted
one who eats volume of a large date Rav Pappa asked:
